title: Add qemu_x86 harness for gateway adapter socket tests
description: Follow-up from project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.4 validation. meshtastic_gateway_adapter was restricted to native_sim because qemu_x86 exits before Zephyr boots: qemu-system-i386 is launched with '-serial unix:/tmp/slip.sock' and fails with 'Failed to connect to /tmp/slip.sock: No such file or directory'. Provide a Twister/qemu harness that creates the expected SLIP socket or otherwise disables that serial dependency, then re-enable qemu_x86 coverage for gateway adapter tests.
notes:
Implementation evidence 2026-07-02:
- Split meshtastic_gateway_adapter into two Twister scenarios: native_sim keeps production Meshtastic BLE Kconfig path (CONFIG_BT=y, CONFIG_BT_PERIPHERAL=y, CONFIG_BT_MAX_CONN=1, CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE=y); qemu_x86 uses CONFIG_NET_TEST=y plus CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_GATEWAY_ADAPTER_TEST_SURFACE=y so Zephyr does not request qemu SLIP/TAP or Bluetooth serial sockets.
- Added ztest-only LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_GATEWAY_ADAPTER_TEST_SURFACE to expose inbound CoAP resources without enabling production BLE GATT. Test-surface path shares CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_GATEWAY_MESSAGE_QUEUE_DEPTH and CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE_MAX_PAYLOAD=200 with Meshtastic BLE.
- CMake Bluetooth warning waiver is guarded with if(TARGET drivers__bluetooth) so qemu builds without Bluetooth do not reference a missing Zephyr target.

Code review evidence:
- Noether initial/re-review: no P0/P1/P2; CONFIG_NET_TEST suppresses qemu SLIP/TAP and integration_platforms preserves coverage.
- Carver initial P2: platform_allow alone did not ensure qemu integration coverage; fixed by scenario/integration_platforms split; re-review clean.
- Linnaeus third review: no P0/P1/P2 on initial qemu harness.
- Hubble P2: single qemu-safe config dropped production Meshtastic BLE coverage on native_sim; fixed by splitting native/qemu scenarios.
- Wegener P2: qemu test-surface path used default app payload cap 176 instead of Meshtastic 200; fixed by extending gateway Kconfig default to test surface.
- Nietzsche re-review: no P0/P1/P2 after scenario split.
- McClintock final re-review: no P0/P1/P2 after payload-cap fix.
- Fermat qemu validation/review: qemu build/run passed with CONFIG_NET_TEST=y, CONFIG_NET_L2_DUMMY=y, CONFIG_BT unset, CONFIG_NET_SLIP_TAP unset, and no qemu /tmp/slip.sock or Bluetooth serial socket.

Validation evidence:
- EC2 i-09a3b01a4d5428ed4 in us-east-2a with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr; clean remote clone worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-xehk-validate5; primary cached worktree preserved dirty/behind.
- Command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out-xehk5 --inline-logs --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen
- Result: 2 test scenarios / 4 platform instances selected, 2 intended configurations passed, 2 static skips, 0 failed, 0 errored, 0 warnings, 62 test cases executed, 62 skipped, 79.34s.
- Passed cases: net.lichen.meshtastic_gateway_adapter.native on native_sim; net.lichen.meshtastic_gateway_adapter.qemu on qemu_x86.
- Generated native_sim config evidence: CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE=y, CONFIG_BT=y, CONFIG_BT_PERIPHERAL=y, CONFIG_BT_MAX_CONN=1, CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE_MAX_PAYLOAD=200, CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_GATEWAY_MESSAGE_QUEUE_DEPTH=3, test surface unset.
- Generated qemu_x86 config evidence: CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_GATEWAY_ADAPTER_TEST_SURFACE=y, CONFIG_NET_TEST=y, CONFIG_NET_L2_DUMMY=y, CONFIG_NET_SLIP_TAP unset, CONFIG_BT unset, CONFIG_LICHEN_APP_INTERFACE_MAX_PAYLOAD=200, CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_GATEWAY_MESSAGE_QUEUE_DEPTH=3.
- Size evidence: native_sim text=245269 data=7135 bss=74924 dec=327328; qemu_x86 text=205052 data=8048 bss=154700 dec=367800.
- Local check: git diff --check passed.
close_reason: Implemented and validated qemu_x86 gateway adapter harness. native_sim retains production Meshtastic BLE coverage; qemu_x86 runs qemu-safe test surface with NET_TEST and fake BLE, no /tmp/slip.sock or Bluetooth serial socket dependency. EC2 Twister passed for both intended scenarios with 0 failures/errors/warnings.
